1.author: Ahmed Ali
1.text: I CALL TO witness the sun and his early morning splendour,

1.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
1.text: By oath of the sun and its light

1.author: Arberry
1.text: By the sun and his morning brightness

1.author: Daryabadi
1.text: By the sun and his morning bright-ness,

1.author: Hilali & Khan
1.text: And by the sun and its brightness;

1.author: Itani
1.text: By the sun and its radiance.

1.author: Maududi
1.text: By the sun and its heat and brightness,

1.author: Pickthall
1.text: By the sun and his brightness,

1.author: Qarai
1.text: By the sun and her forenoon splendour,

1.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
1.text: By the sun and its midmorning,

1.author: Saheeh International
1.text: By the sun and its brightness

1.author: Sarwar
1.text: By the sun and its noon-time brightness,

1.author: Shakir
1.text: I swear by the sun and its brilliance,

1.author: Wahiduddin Khan
1.text: By the sun and its rising brightness

1.author: Yusuf Ali
1.text: By the Sun and his (glorious) splendour;

2.author: Ahmed Ali
2.text: And the moon as she follows in his wake,

2.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
2.text: And by oath of the moon when it follows the sun

2.author: Arberry
2.text: and by the moon when she follows him,

2.author: Daryabadi
2.text: By the moon, when she followeth him,

2.author: Hilali & Khan
2.text: And by the moon as it follows it (the sun);

2.author: Itani
2.text: And the moon as it follows it.

2.author: Maududi
2.text: and by the moon as it follows it;

2.author: Mubarakpuri
2.text: By the moon as it Talaha.

2.author: Pickthall
2.text: And the moon when she followeth him,

2.author: Qarai
2.text: by the moon when he follows her,

2.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
2.text: by the moon, which follows it,

2.author: Saheeh International
2.text: And [by] the moon when it follows it

2.author: Sarwar
2.text: by the moon when it follows the sun,

2.author: Shakir
2.text: And the moon when it follows the sun,

2.author: Wahiduddin Khan
2.text: and by the moon as it follows it,

2.author: Yusuf Ali
2.text: By the Moon as she follows him;

3.author: Ahmed Ali
3.text: The day when it reveals his radiance,

3.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
3.text: And by oath of the day when it reveals it

3.author: Arberry
3.text: and by the day when it displays him

3.author: Daryabadi
3.text: By the day when it glorifieth him.

3.author: Hilali & Khan
3.text: And by the day as it shows up (the sun's) brightness;

3.author: Itani
3.text: And the day as it reveals it.

3.author: Maududi
3.text: and by the day as it displays the sun's glory,

3.author: Mubarakpuri
3.text: By the day as it Jallaha.

3.author: Pickthall
3.text: And the day when it revealeth him,

3.author: Qarai
3.text: by the day when it reveals her,

3.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
3.text: by the day, when it displays it,

3.author: Saheeh International
3.text: And [by] the day when it displays it

3.author: Sarwar
3.text: by the day when it brightens the earth,

3.author: Shakir
3.text: And the day when it shows it,

3.author: Wahiduddin Khan
3.text: and by the day as it reveals its glory

3.author: Yusuf Ali
3.text: By the Day as it shows up (the Sun's) glory;

4.author: Ahmed Ali
4.text: The night when it covers him over,

4.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
4.text: And by oath of the night when it hides it

4.author: Arberry
4.text: and by the night when it enshrouds him!

4.author: Daryabadi
4.text: By the night; when it envelopeth him,

4.author: Hilali & Khan
4.text: And by the night as it conceals it (the sun);

4.author: Itani
4.text: And the night as it conceals it.

4.author: Maududi
4.text: and by the night as it envelopes the sun;

4.author: Mubarakpuri
4.text: By the night as it Yaghshaha.

4.author: Pickthall
4.text: And the night when it enshroudeth him,

4.author: Qarai
4.text: by the night when it covers her,

4.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
4.text: by the night, when it envelops it!

4.author: Saheeh International
4.text: And [by] the night when it covers it

4.author: Sarwar
4.text: by the night when it covers the earth with darkness,

4.author: Shakir
4.text: And the night when it draws a veil over it,

4.author: Wahiduddin Khan
4.text: and by the night when it draws a veil over it,

4.author: Yusuf Ali
4.text: By the Night as it conceals it;

5.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
5.text: And by oath of the heaven and by oath of Him Who made it.

5.author: Arberry
5.text: By the heaven and That which built it

5.author: Daryabadi
5.text: By the heaven and Him Who builded it,

5.author: Hilali & Khan
5.text: And by the heaven and Him Who built it;

5.author: Itani
5.text: And the sky and He who built it.

5.author: Maududi
5.text: and by the sky and by Him Who made it;

5.author: Mubarakpuri
5.text: By the heaven and Ma Banaha.

5.author: Pickthall
5.text: And the heaven and Him Who built it,

5.author: Qarai
5.text: by the sky and Him who built it,

5.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
5.text: By the heaven and Who built it,

5.author: Saheeh International
5.text: And [by] the sky and He who constructed it

5.author: Sarwar
5.text: by the heavens and that (Power) which established them,

5.author: Shakir
5.text: And the heaven and Him Who made it,

5.author: Wahiduddin Khan
5.text: by the sky and how He built it

5.author: Yusuf Ali
5.text: By the Firmament and its (wonderful) structure;

6.author: Ahmed Ali
6.text: The earth and its spreading out,

6.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
6.text: And by oath of the earth and by oath of Him Who spread it.

6.author: Arberry
6.text: and by the earth and That which extended it!

6.author: Daryabadi
6.text: By the earth and Him Who spread it forth,

6.author: Hilali & Khan
6.text: And by the earth and Him Who spread it,

6.author: Itani
6.text: And the earth and He who spread it.

6.author: Maududi
6.text: and by the earth and by Him Who stretched it out;

6.author: Mubarakpuri
6.text: By the earth and Ma Tahhaha.

6.author: Pickthall
6.text: And the earth and Him Who spread it,

6.author: Qarai
6.text: by the earth and Him who spread it,

6.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
6.text: by the earth and Who spread it,

6.author: Saheeh International
6.text: And [by] the earth and He who spread it

6.author: Sarwar
6.text: by the earth and that (Power) which spread it out

6.author: Shakir
6.text: And the earth and Him Who extended it,

6.author: Wahiduddin Khan
6.text: and by the earth and how He spread it,

6.author: Yusuf Ali
6.text: By the Earth and its (wide) expanse:

7.author: Ahmed Ali
7.text: The soul and how it was integrated

7.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
7.text: And by oath of the soul and by oath of Him Who made it proper.

7.author: Arberry
7.text: By the soul, and That which shaped it

7.author: Daryabadi
7.text: By the soul and Him Who propertioned it,

7.author: Hilali & Khan
7.text: And by Nafs (Adam or a person or a soul, etc.), and Him Who perfected him in proportion;

7.author: Itani
7.text: And the soul and He who proportioned it.

7.author: Maududi
7.text: and by the soul and by Him Who perfectly proportioned it,

7.author: Mubarakpuri
7.text: By Nafs, and Ma Sawwaha (Who apportioned it).

7.author: Pickthall
7.text: And a soul and Him Who perfected it

7.author: Qarai
7.text: by the soul and Him who fashioned it,

7.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
7.text: by the soul and Who shaped it

7.author: Saheeh International
7.text: And [by] the soul and He who proportioned it

7.author: Sarwar
7.text: and by the soul and that (Power) which designed it

7.author: Shakir
7.text: And the soul and Him Who made it perfect,

7.author: Wahiduddin Khan
7.text: by the soul and how He formed it,

7.author: Yusuf Ali
7.text: By the Soul, and the proportion and order given to it;

8.author: Ahmed Ali
8.text: And given the faculty of knowing what is disruptive and what is intrinsic to it.

8.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
8.text: And inspired in it the knowledge of its sins and its piety.

8.author: Arberry
8.text: and inspired it to lewdness and godfearing!

8.author: Daryabadi
8.text: And inspired it with the wickedness thereof and the piety thereof,

8.author: Hilali & Khan
8.text: Then He showed him what is wrong for him and what is right for him;

8.author: Itani
8.text: And inspired it with its wickedness and its righteousness.

8.author: Maududi
8.text: and imbued it with (the consciousness of) its evil and its piety:

8.author: Mubarakpuri
8.text: Then He showed it its Fujur and its Taqwa.

8.author: Pickthall
8.text: And inspired it (with conscience of) what is wrong for it and (what is) right for it.

8.author: Qarai
8.text: and inspired it with [discernment between] its virtues and vices:

8.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
8.text: and inspired it with its sin and its piety,

8.author: Saheeh International
8.text: And inspired it [with discernment of] its wickedness and its righteousness,

8.author: Sarwar
8.text: and inspired it with knowledge of evil and piety,

8.author: Shakir
8.text: Then He inspired it to understand what is right and wrong for it;

8.author: Wahiduddin Khan
8.text: then inspired it to understand what was right and wrong for it.

8.author: Yusuf Ali
8.text: And its enlightenment as to its wrong and its right;-

9.author: Ahmed Ali
9.text: He who nourishes it will surely be successful,

9.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
9.text: Indeed successful is the one who made it pure.

9.author: Arberry
9.text: Prosperous is he who purifies it,

9.author: Daryabadi
9.text: Surely blissful is he who hath cleansed, it,

9.author: Hilali & Khan
9.text: Indeed he succeeds who purifies his ownself (i.e. obeys and performs all that Allah ordered, by following the true Faith of Islamic Monotheism and by doing righteous good deeds).

9.author: Itani
9.text: Successful is he who purifies it.

9.author: Maududi
9.text: He who purifies it will prosper,

9.author: Mubarakpuri
9.text: Indeed he succeeds who purifies it.

9.author: Pickthall
9.text: He is indeed successful who causeth it to grow,

9.author: Qarai
9.text: one who purifies it is felicitous,

9.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
9.text: prosperous is he who purified it,

9.author: Saheeh International
9.text: He has succeeded who purifies it,

9.author: Sarwar
9.text: those who purify their souls will certainly have everlasting happiness

9.author: Shakir
9.text: He will indeed be successful who purifies it,

9.author: Wahiduddin Khan
9.text: He who purifies it will indeed be successful,

9.author: Yusuf Ali
9.text: Truly he succeeds that purifies it,

10.author: Ahmed Ali
10.text: And he who confines it will surely come to grief.

10.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
10.text: And indeed failed is the one who covered it in sin.

10.author: Arberry
10.text: and failed has he who seduces it.

10.author: Daryabadi
10.text: And miserable is he who hath buried it.

10.author: Hilali & Khan
10.text: And indeed he fails who corrupts his ownself (i.e. disobeys what Allah has ordered by rejecting the true Faith of Islamic Monotheism or by following polytheism, etc. or by doing every kind of evil wicked deeds).

10.author: Itani
10.text: Failing is he who corrupts it.

10.author: Maududi
10.text: and he who suppresses it will be ruined.

10.author: Mubarakpuri
10.text: And indeed he fails who Dassaha.

10.author: Pickthall
10.text: And he is indeed a failure who stunteth it.

10.author: Qarai
10.text: and one who betrays it fails.

10.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
10.text: and failed is he who buried it!

10.author: Saheeh International
10.text: And he has failed who instills it [with corruption].

10.author: Sarwar
10.text: and those who corrupt their souls will certainly be deprived (of happiness).

10.author: Shakir
10.text: And he will indeed fail who corrupts it.

10.author: Wahiduddin Khan
10.text: and he who corrupts it is sure to fail.

10.author: Yusuf Ali
10.text: And he fails that corrupts it!

11.author: Ahmed Ali
11.text: The Thamud denied (the truth) in their perverseness

11.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
11.text: The tribe of Thamud denied with rebellion.

11.author: Arberry
11.text: Thamood cried lies in their insolence

11.author: Hilali & Khan
11.text: Thamud (people) denied (their Prophet) through their transgression (by rejecting the true Faith of Islamic Monotheism, and by following polytheism, and by committing every kind of sin).

11.author: Maududi
11.text: In their presumptuous insolence the Thamud called the Truth a lie

11.author: Mubarakpuri
11.text: Thamud denied through their trans- gression.

11.author: Pickthall
11.text: (The tribe of) Thamud denied (the truth) in their rebellious pride,

11.author: Qarai
11.text: The [people of] Thamud denied [Allah’s signs] out of their rebellion,

11.author: Saheeh International
11.text: Thamud denied [their prophet] by reason of their transgression,

11.author: Sarwar
11.text: The people of Thamud rejected (the truth) as a result of their rebelliousness

11.author: Shakir
11.text: Samood gave the lie (to the truth) in their inordinacy,

11.author: Wahiduddin Khan
11.text: The Thamud tribe rejected the truth because of their arrogance,

11.author: Yusuf Ali
11.text: The Thamud (people) rejected (their prophet) through their inordinate wrong-doing,

12.author: Ahmed Ali
12.text: When among them the great wretch arose,

12.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
12.text: When the most wicked among them stood up in defiance.

12.author: Arberry
12.text: when the most wretched of them uprose,

12.author: Daryabadi
12.text: What time the greatest wretch of them rose up.

12.author: Hilali & Khan
12.text: When the most wicked man among them went forth (to kill the she-camel).

12.author: Itani
12.text: When it followed its most wicked.

12.author: Maududi
12.text: when their arch-criminal rose up in rage.

12.author: Mubarakpuri
12.text: When their most wicked went forth.

12.author: Pickthall
12.text: When the basest of them broke forth

12.author: Qarai
12.text: when the most wretched of them rose up.

12.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
12.text: when the most wicked of them broke forth,

12.author: Saheeh International
12.text: When the most wretched of them was sent forth.

12.author: Sarwar
12.text: when the most corrupt of them incited them (to commit evil).

12.author: Shakir
12.text: When the most unfortunate of them broke forth with

12.author: Wahiduddin Khan
12.text: when the most wicked man among them rose up.

12.author: Yusuf Ali
12.text: Behold, the most wicked man among them was deputed (for impiety).

13.author: Ahmed Ali
13.text: And the apostle of God had to tell them: "This is God's she-camel, let her drink."

13.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
13.text: So the Noble Messenger of Allah said to them, “Stay away from the she camel of Allah, and from its turn to drink.”

13.author: Arberry
13.text: then the Messenger of God said to them, 'The She-camel of God; let her drink!'

13.author: Daryabadi
13.text: Then the apostle of Allah said Unto them: beware of the she-camel of Allah and her drink.

13.author: Hilali & Khan
13.text: But the Messenger of Allah [Salih (Saleh)] said to them: "Be cautious! Fear the evil end. That is the she-camel of Allah! (Do not harm it) and bar it not from having its drink!"

13.author: Itani
13.text: The messenger of God said to them, “This is the she-camel of God, so let her drink.”

13.author: Maududi
13.text: Then Allah's Messenger warned them: “Hands off the she-camel and her drink!”

13.author: Mubarakpuri
13.text: But the Messenger of Allah said to them: "Be cautious! That is the she-camel of Allah! (Do not harm it) and (bar it not from having) its drink!"

13.author: Pickthall
13.text: And the messenger of Allah said: It is the she-camel of Allah, so let her drink!

13.author: Qarai
13.text: The apostle of Allah had told them, ‘This is Allah’s she-camel, let her drink!’

13.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
13.text: the Messenger of Allah said to them: '(This is) the shecamel of Allah, let her drink'

13.author: Saheeh International
13.text: And the messenger of Allah [Salih] said to them, "[Do not harm] the she-camel of Allah or [prevent her from] her drink."

13.author: Sarwar
13.text: The Messenger of God told them, "This is a she-camel, belonging to God. Do not deprive her of her share of water".

13.author: Shakir
13.text: So Allah's apostle said to them (Leave alone) Allah's she-camel, and (give) her (to) drink.

13.author: Wahiduddin Khan
13.text: Then the messenger of God said to them, "This is God's she-camel. Let her drink."

13.author: Yusuf Ali
13.text: But the Messenger of Allah said to them: "It is a She-camel of Allah! And (bar her not from) having her drink!"

13.author: Transliteration
13.text: Faqala lahum rasoolu Allahi naqata Allahi wasuqyaha

14.author: Ahmed Ali
14.text: But they called him a liar and hamstrung her. So God obliterated them for their crime, and (destroyed) all of them alike.

14.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
14.text: In response they belied him, and hamstrung the she camel – so Allah put ruin over them because of their sins and flattened their dwellings.

14.author: Arberry
14.text: But they cried him lies, and hamstrung her, so their Lord crushed them for their sin, and leveled them:

14.author: Daryabadi
14.text: Then they belied him, and they hamstrung her; wherefore their Lord overwhelmed them for their crime, and made it equal.

14.author: Hilali & Khan
14.text: Then they denied him and they killed it. So their Lord destroyed them because of their sin, and made them equal in destruction (i.e. all grades of people, rich and poor, strong and weak, etc.)!

14.author: Itani
14.text: But they called him a liar, and hamstrung her. So their Lord crushed them for their sin, and leveled it.

14.author: Maududi
14.text: But they rejected his statement as a lie and hamstrung the she-camel. For that crime their Lord rumbled down upon them, utterly razing them to the ground.

14.author: Mubarakpuri
14.text: Then they denied him and they killed it. So their Lord destroyed them because of their sin, Fasawwaha!

14.author: Pickthall
14.text: But they denied him, and they hamstrung her, so Allah doomed them for their sin and rased (their dwellings).

14.author: Qarai
14.text: But they impugned him and hamstrung her. So their Lord took them unawares by night because of their sin, and levelled it.

14.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
14.text: But they belied him, and hamstrung her. So their Lord crushed them for their sin and leveled it (their village).

14.author: Saheeh International
14.text: But they denied him and hamstrung her. So their Lord brought down upon them destruction for their sin and made it equal [upon all of them].

14.author: Sarwar
14.text: However, they rejected him and slew her. So their Lord completely destroyed them and their city for their sins.

14.author: Shakir
14.text: But they called him a liar and slaughtered her, therefore their Lord crushed them for their sin and levelled them (with the ground).

14.author: Wahiduddin Khan
14.text: But they gave him the lie, and hamstrung the she-camel. So their Lord destroyed them for their crime and razed their city to the ground.

14.author: Yusuf Ali
14.text: Then they rejected him (as a false prophet), and they hamstrung her. So their Lord, on account of their crime, obliterated their traces and made them equal (in destruction, high and low)!

14.author: Transliteration
14.text: Fakaththaboohu faAAaqarooha fadamdama AAalayhim rabbuhum bithanbihim fasawwaha

15.author: Ahmed Ali
15.text: He does not fear the consequence.

15.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
15.text: And He does not fear its coming behind (to take vengeance).

15.author: Arberry
15.text: and He fears not the issue thereof.

15.author: Daryabadi
15.text: And He feared not the consequence thereof

15.author: Hilali & Khan
15.text: And He (Allah) feared not the consequences thereof.

15.author: Itani
15.text: And He does not fear its sequel.

15.author: Maududi
15.text: He has no fear of its sequel.

15.author: Mubarakpuri
15.text: And He feared not the consequences thereof.

15.author: Pickthall
15.text: He dreadeth not the sequel (of events).

15.author: Qarai
15.text: And He does not fear its outcome.

15.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
15.text: He does not fear the result (of their destruction).

15.author: Saheeh International
15.text: And He does not fear the consequence thereof.

15.author: Sarwar
15.text: God is not afraid of the result of what He had decreed.

15.author: Shakir
15.text: And He fears not its consequence.

15.author: Wahiduddin Khan
15.text: He did not fear the consequences.

15.author: Yusuf Ali
15.text: And for Him is no fear of its consequences.
